title: Lannea grandis

The genus Lannea is a group of plants commonly known as wild plum trees or soapberrys. Lannea grandis is native to tropical Africa, where it grows in a variety of environments. The classification of Lannea grandis is further broken down into its species and genus levels, with its scientific name indicating its unique characteristics. uses: Lannea grandis is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
